title: Salicornia Germination on Agar Plate
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"aite7","text":"This method describes the steps to germinate seeds of various Salicornia species under sterile conditions for different purposes; germination of wild-collected seeds, tissue culture and where disease/pest-free plants are required for hydronic experiments. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":62,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"2gjf1","text":"It is advisable to perform all the steps under sterile conditions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"fi84k","text":"Murashige and Skoog Basal Medium - Sigma-Aldrich","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8poip","text":"Sucrose - Sigma-Aldrich","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1gmup","text":"NaCl ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dks55","text":"Preservative for Plant Tissue Culture Media (PPM) - Plant Cell Technology","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a310r","text":"Agar - Sigma-Aldrich","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7oa7","text":"Ethanol Absolute (\u003e99.5%) EMPARTA ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"961fr","text":"Bleach (household grade)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fam8n","text":"TWEEN 20 - TermoFisher","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"27miu","text":"Gibberellic acid - Sigma-Aldrich","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Salicornia medium germination. One liter of medium is enough for approximately 20 Petri dishes (100mm x 100mm x 15mm). A B C D 1 Component 500 mL 1000 mL 2000 mL 2 Murashige and Skoog Basal Medium (MS with vitamins) M0404-10L (Sigma Aldrich) 2.2 g 4.4 g 8.8 g 3 Sucrose 3% (w/v) 15 g 30 g 60 g 4 100 mM NaCl* 2.922 g 5.844 g 11.688 g 5 Preservative for Plant Tissue Culture Media (PPM)** 0.5 mL 1 mL 2 mL 6 Adjust pH 5.8 7 Agar 0.5% (w/v) 2.5 g 5 g 10 g *NaCl is optional and dependent on the aim of the project. **PPM is optional; it is used to prevent microbial contamination and recommended for wild-collected seeds.
Store the Petri dishes at room temperature for short-term storage or at 4 °C for long-term storage.
Place the seeds in a 2 mL Eppendorf tube. Add 1 ml 70% (v/v) Ethanol and mix for 1 minute. Remove the ethanol by pipetting carefully to avoid discarding the seeds.

Prepare a solution containing 20% (v/v) bleach and 0.2% (v/v) TWEEN 20. Add 1 mL of this solution to the seeds and incubate for 10 minutes, mixing occasionally. After incubation, remove the solution and wash the seeds four times with Milli-Q water. Carefully remove the supernatant by pipetting to avoid discarding the seeds.

Place the sterilized seeds on agar plates. Use forceps to place them one by one, or use a 1000 µL pipette to distribute them onto the agar. Cover the Petri dishes with aluminum foil during the first 24 hours. If germination is low, add one drop (50 µL) of 500 µM Gibberellic Acid directly onto the seeds and keep them in the dark for 24 hours to enhance germination. 500 µM Gibberellic Acid = 17 mg/100 mL water Keep the plates in a controlled-environment growth chamber for approximately two weeks. Grow the seeds under a 12-hour light / 12-hour dark photoperiod at 28 °C. Salicornia bigelovii seeds after two weeks on petri dish.
Once the seedlings reach a height of 1 cm on the agar plates, they are ready to be transferred to soil or hydroponics.
creator: Lina Maria Caceres Leal
